meaning: adj.骑在骆驼背上的adv.骑在骆驼背上地n.骆驼背,一种翻新的橡胶
examples: They dry the meat and keep the ivory and transport it on camelback.
Mounted police are police who do patrols on horseback or camelback.
This site for this project has one main view to the east towards Camelback Mountain.
The resort basks in the shadow of Camelback Mountain, named for its resemblance to a kneeling camel.
Seasoned climbers can take on the challenge to climb Camelback Mountain and enjoy the view from the top.
